input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-10T01:02:08.646Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page follows the system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#512BD4, Dark=#7E5CE5}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` reacts to `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` automatically — no event wiring needed. When the OS theme changes, every bound property updates in real time.

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme`

The equivalent in code-behind or C# markup:

```csharp
// Extension method on any BindableObject
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);
//                 property                  light value   dark value

var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
                 Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),
                 Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E"));
```

`SetAppTheme(property, lightValue, darkValue)` does the same thing as the XAML markup extension.

## Tips

- **Resource dictionaries** — define theme colors once and reference them:
  ```xml
  <!-- In App.xaml or a ResourceDictionary -->
  <Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}</Color>

  <!-- Usage -->
  <ContentPage BackgroundColor="{StaticResource PageBg}" />
  ```
- **Manual override** — force a theme with `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;`. Set it to `AppTheme.Unspecified` to revert to system default.
- **Detect current theme** — read `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (returns `AppTheme.Light` or `AppTheme.Dark`).
- **React to changes** — subscribe to `Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged` if you need imperative logic beyond binding.
